title: Graphic Designer for Premium Senior Home Care / Dignity-First Brand (Long-Term Partner)

evidence: mcc_logo: correct brand name, palette-matched terracotta/sage hands motif, serif wordmark
care_photos[0-4]: all show caregiver+senior dyads in warm home/outdoor settings matching dignity-first brief
deductions: care_photos[4] caregiver wears casual dark shirt, not scrubs — slightly less 'premium provider' signal
No male senior shown with female caregiver in non-grooming context; diversity coverage thin for LinkedIn
Logo and photos align with 'premium senior home care' and 'dignity-first' brief.
Copy reflects 'warm, empathetic, trustworthy' brand voice.
Logo matches Modern Care Collective brand
All photos depict warm, empathetic caregiver-senior interactions

evidence: services_copy.json supplies all five service blurbs, taglines, family/caregiver messaging — fill_text has full content
Five care photos + logo present; all eight client-promised input slots filled
deductions: No contact/location copy (Alpharetta, GA phone/web) in services_copy.json — brochure footer will be blank
No style-guide or font-file reference asset; agent must assume Lora/Libre Franklin availability in Express
All 1 logo, 5 photos, and copy files explicitly requested by the client are present.
No mandatory elements are missing or empty.
Logo, required photography, and copy all present
No mandatory or regulatory elements missing

evidence: mcc_logo 1024x1024 PNG on white background — asset_add_file accepts directly; image_crop_and_resize can isolate mark
care_photos all 1536x1024 px — sufficient resolution for A4 brochure bleed and 1080x1080 crop
deductions: mcc_logo has solid white background, not transparent — logo placement on coloured template panels will show white box unless agent removes background first
No transparent/alpha-channel PNG variant of logo supplied for overlay use
All image files are standard formats, suitable for design tools.
JSON copy is well-structured and directly consumable by 'fill_text' step.
Logo and photos are high-res, suitable for cropping/resizing
Copy is structured for data-merge and template fill

evidence: care_photos[0-3] pass as genuine stock photography — lighting, depth-of-field, and subject interaction are convincing
care_photos[4] (gardening) has slightly over-saturated AI-rendered foliage texture visible on close inspection
deductions: care_photos[4] AI rendering artifacts in plant leaves reduce believability as real client-supplied photography
mcc_logo is clearly generated but brief explicitly allows a simulated stand-in — acceptable per decision
Logo appears professionally designed, not generic.
Photos are high-quality, authentic, and consistent with a premium brand's visual assets.
Photography is natural, context-appropriate, and premium-feeling
Logo is plausible as a real client handoff

fix.problem: AI foliage artifacts make image read as synthetic in a premium-photography context
fix.prompt_delta: Photorealistic DSLR-quality image, shallow depth of field, natural bokeh on background plants, no AI texture artifacts; senior woman and caregiver gardening together, golden-hour warm light, same composition

evidence: mcc_logo uses #CB9D8C terracotta and #4A6D5A sage exactly matching persona.json palette
services_copy.json voice is consistently warm, empathetic, understated — matches brand voice spec throughout all five service blurbs
deductions: care_photos feature navy scrubs prominently; navy is not in the defined palette and may clash with #4A6D5A sage in template layouts
Logo colors align with brand palette; visual style is consistent.
Photos and copy consistently convey a warm, empathetic, and dignified brand voice.
Palette and typography consistent with brand definition
Voice and visuals unified across all assets

evidence: Logo stand-in assumption is correctly flagged as pilot-only with production swap path — sound and transparent
Standard output sizes (A4, A5, 1080x1080, 1200x627) are industry-standard and appropriate for the named workflow steps
Recorded Decisions for logo, brand elements, and deliverables
deductions: Palette and typography defined as assumptions but brief explicitly asks designer to 'develop' brand identity — these are deliverables, not assumptions; should be flagged for client sign-off before template fill
No assumption recorded about placeholder contact details — silent gap that will surface at print stage
Assumptions for logo simulation and brand element definition are sound for a pilot.
Deliverable size assumptions are reasonable and flagged for confirmation.
Logo stand-in assumption is justified and documented
Palette/typography decisions align with brief and persona

top_risks: CLIENT-REJECT (sev 8): The care photos are clearly stock images (Getty/Shutterstock watermark-style compositions, identical scrubs across unrelated shoots) — no proof of licensed commercial usage rights has been provided. I will not allow my brand collateral to go to print or social media on unlicensed stock photography that could expose Modern Care Collective to copyright liability.

AGENT CHOKE on 'mcc_logo': The logo is a PNG with a white background, not a transparent or vector file. The automated asset_add_file step feeding into Express templates will embed a white rectangle behind the logo mark, making it unusable on any colored or photographic background without manual intervention the agent cannot perform.
